Container.Name: wyoming-kokoro
Container.Overview: [b]Wyoming Kokoro (TTS)[/b] — neural text-to-speech via Kokoro-ONNX exposed over the Wyoming protocol so Home Assistant can use it as a TTS engine. CPU-only; pairs with [i]wyoming-parakeet[/i] for a full local Assist pipeline. [br] [b]Model downloads on first run[/b] (~80 MB for fp32). Change the [i]Model Variant[/i] env to [i]int8[/i] for a smaller/faster model on low-power hardware. [br] [b]To use in Home Assistant:[/b] Settings → Devices & services → Add integration → [i]Wyoming Protocol[/i] → host = Unraid IP, port = 10200. [br] Packages [i]chiabre/wyoming-kokoro[/i] (MIT) into a reproducible image. Source: [url=https://github.com/iknox/wyoming-kokoro-cpu]iknox/wyoming-kokoro-cpu[/url].
Container.Description: CPU-only Kokoro TTS over the Wyoming protocol. Default voice af_heart (see upstream README for the full voice catalog — over 50 voices across English, Spanish, French, Italian, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, Chinese).

Config.Name: Wyoming Port
Config.Description: Wyoming protocol TCP port — Home Assistant connects here (tcp://UNRAID-IP:10200).

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Model + voices cache. Files auto-download on first run (~80 MB).

Config.Name: KOKORO_MODEL
Config.Description: Model variant: fp32 (CPU, default, best quality), int8 (smaller, faster on edge hardware).
